summary: Recent changes in the image decoders section of Firefox have improved the handling of JPEG XL (JXL), a modern image format that offers better compression and quality. The decoder now displays **progressive JXL images much sooner**, enhancing the user experience by reducing loading times for images. Additionally, the updates ensure that color management is correctly applied before the image's alpha (transparency) is adjusted, which helps maintain consistent colors. Improvements also include better pixel conversion across different formats and infrastructure improvements for more efficient processing. Overall, these enhancements allow Firefox to render images more quickly and accurately, contributing to a smoother browsing experience.
